data.title: Activist investor David Lazar to invest $3M in LQR House
data.description:
• LQR House (NASDAQ:LQR) said on Wednesday that it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with activist investor David Lazar. Under the agreement, Lazar has committed to providing $3M in funding to LQR House.
  • It is expected that by October 16, Lazar will make an initial payment of $606,000 in exchange for approximately 1.1M shares of LQR House common stock.
  • The balance of $2.4M will be funded at the final closing, subject to certain closing conditions, including shareholder approval.
  • Upon completion of the full $3M funding, Lazar will also receive warrants to purchase 10.9M shares of the company's common stock at an initial exercise price of $0.55 per share.
  • As part of the transaction, Lazar has been appointed to the LQR House Board of Directors and as President.
  • The company said, following the final closing, Lazar is expected to become the company's largest shareholder, with significant voting influence over the company.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures muted after Wall Street closes lower on tech sell off
data.description:
Stock index futures traded cautiously, a day after chip stocks slumped on a disappointing outlook from ASML (ASML).

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.01%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 3.95%.

Wall Street closed in the red on Tuesday, as chip stocks took it on the chin following disappointing earnings from Dutch giant ASML (ASML) and news that the U.S. was mulling further export caps.

"Some rare weakness has come through in risk over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 falling back from its record high the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

ASML (ASML) shares were down 4% in premarket trade, after the firm said it expects 2025 net sales forecast to be between €30B to €35B (mid-point of €32.5B). The Dutch company also missed its third-quarter bookings estimates.

Morgan Stanley (MS) delivered a beat on both its top and bottom line in its third quarter, as banks continued to shine.

U.S. import and export prices fell more than expected in September.

data.title: Brookdale initiated with Buy at Jefferies on 'golden age for senior housing'
data.description:
Brookdale Senior Living (NYSE:BKD) was initiated with a Buy rating, and a price target of $8, at Jefferies, citing the "golden age for senior housing" industry is here.

The stock was trading 2.96% higher Wednesday pre-market at $6.25, and has gained 49.14% in the last one year.

BKD is the largest owner and operator of senior living communities in the U.S., the investment bank and financial services company said in a research note.

"As these demographic trends translate to increased demand for sr housing, expect BKD, the leading player in the space, to see growth and earnings acceleration that has a long tail, that we believe aren't baked into the stock yet," noted analyst Brian Tanquilut.

The recent slowdown in new construction should translate to increased occupancy and margins, while the company's success in stabilizing labor costs and restructuring leases should translate into growth and expansion, according to Jefferies.

BKD's recent lease renewals could be positive catalysts, Jefferies noted.

Jefferies rating aligns with the average sell-side analyst rating. Seeking Alpha authors as well as the Quant Rating system grades the stock as Hold.

"BKD remains a hold in my opinion given the fact there has been little to no change in the fundamental economics of the business after its Q2 numbers," said SA contributor Zach Bristow.

data.title: Pinnacle Financial Partners declares $0.22 dividend
data.description:

data.title: Diebold Nixdorf receives Moody's credit rating upgrade with positive outlook
data.description:
• Diebold Nixdorf (NYSE:DBD) Wednesday said that Moody's has recently upgraded its credit rating for the company to B3 from Caa1.
  • Moody's outlook remained positive.
  • Moody's also noted that the positive outlook reflects expectations that Diebold Nixdorf will continue to make progress towards linearizing its quarterly cash flows.
  • DBD +1.18% premarket to $46.39.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: A10 Networks outlines blueprint to secure and deliver AI applications
data.description:
• GenAI and large language models, along with high-power GPUs, are enabling artificial intelligence to drive efficiency, decision-making and to enhance customer experiences.
  • Organizations are implementing their AI infrastructure in the cloud, or on-premises, and accelerating their hybrid cloud strategy.
  • A10 Networks (NYSE:ATEN) is delivering security and AI-based solutions to help customers increase their cyber resilience and harness the power of AI to secure and manage their infrastructures to lower cybersecurity risks, improve user experiences and performance, and simplify their IT infrastructure.
  • Available later this year, A10 Control will allow customers to seamlessly migrate from their existing A10 management consoles to A10 Control and simplify their operations.
  • They will be able to automate A10 device upgrades, manage flexible licenses, monitor system health, all from a single dashboard that provides visibility across all traffic.

data.title: NRG Energy commences offering of senior notes due 2033 and 2034
data.description:
• NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) on Wednesday commenced an offering of $1,500M in aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes in two tranches, consisting of (i) senior notes due 2033 and (ii) senior notes due 2034.
  • The Notes will be guaranteed by each of NRG’s current and future subsidiaries that guarantee indebtedness under NRG’s credit agreement.
  • NRG intends to use the net proceeds from this offering, together with the net proceeds of its proposed new term loan B in an aggregate principal amount of $450 million and cash on hand, to pay the tender price of the tender offer through its subsidiary, APX Group, Inc.

data.title: DZS sells service assurance and Wi-Fi management software portfolio to AXON
data.description:
• DZS (OTC:DZSI) to sell its service assurance and WiFi management software portfolio to AXON Networks for $34 million.
  • The transaction transfers all CloudCheck, Expresse and TruSpeed assets and IP patents to AXON.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Crombie Real Estate Investment Trust to redeem $175M notes
data.description:
• Crombie Real Estate Investment Trust (CRR.UN:CA) said it has issued a notice of redemption to the registered holders of its Series E Notes.
  • The company added the outstanding $175 million aggregate principal amount of the notes will be redeemed in full on October 31.
